What's your
workload profile?

Five honest questions. One surprisingly accurate profile. A few things you can actually do about it.

0 of 5 answered
01 of 05 Boundaries

When asked to take on something new, I can push back thoughtfully — without guilt — if my plate is already full.

What does "not yet" sound like in your voice?

Almost neverAlmost always
02 of 05 Focus

I can tell the difference between work that genuinely moves things forward and work that just keeps me busy.

How much of your week is motion — and how much is real progress?

Almost neverAlmost always
03 of 05 Transparency

I feel comfortable telling my supervisor or team when my workload is at — or beyond — capacity.

When did you last say "I'm stretched" before things got bad?

Almost neverAlmost always
04 of 05 Prioritisation

When something new lands on my plate, I have a way of deciding what moves up and what waits.

Do you have a reliable method — or does it depend on who asked loudest?

Almost neverAlmost always
05 of 05 Clarity

At the end of most weeks, I can name the three things that mattered most.

Think about last week. Could you name them now?

Almost neverAlmost always

Answer all 5 questions to see your profile.

Sources & frameworks

1. Covey, S.R. (1989). The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People. — Urgency vs. importance matrix. Informs Q3 & Q4.

2. Edmondson, A.C. (1999). Psychological safety and learning in work teams. Administrative Science Quarterly. — Informs Q3.

3. Kahneman, D. (2011). Thinking, Fast and Slow. — Completion bias. Informs Q2.

4. Dweck, C.S. (2006). Mindset: The New Psychology of Success. — Growth mindset. Informs Q5.
